[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/subscribe/SubscribeProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: validate()] Can an unprivileged external user configure a `GrpcSubscriberProperties` with `retry.maxAttempts=Long.MAX_VALUE` (the default) and `retry.maxBackoff=Duration.ofSeconds(8L)` to cause the gRPC subscriber to retry indefinitely on a deliberately broken connection, consuming resources while never successfully monitoring staking reward transfers?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/subscribe/SubscribeProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: validate()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the `grpc.forEach((name, property) -> property.setName(name))` call in `validate()` to overwrite a legitimately configured scenario name with a crafted name that causes the gRPC subscriber to connect to a wrong mirror node endpoint, missing staking reward transfer events?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/subscribe/SubscribeProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: validate()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the absence of a maximum `statusFrequency` constraint (only `@DurationMin(seconds=1L)` is enforced) to set `statusFrequency` to `Duration.ofDays(365)`, causing the monitor to log staking reward subscriber status only once per year and hiding ongoing reward theft from operators?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/subscribe/SubscribeProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: validate()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the `@Valid` annotation on the `grpc` and `rest` maps in `SubscribeProperties` to trigger cascading validation failures in nested `GrpcSubscriberProperties` or `RestSubscriberProperties`, causing the monitor to fail to start and leaving staking reward monitoring offline during the startup failure window?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/subscribe/SubscribeProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: validate()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the `Sets.union()` operation in `validate()` to create a scenario where the union of `grpc.keySet()` and `rest.keySet()` contains a blank string that passes `StringUtils.isBlank()` check due to Unicode whitespace characters not recognized by the implementation, registering a phantom subscriber?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/subscribe/SubscribeProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: validate()] Can an unprivileged external user configure the `grpc` map with a scenario whose `topicId` expression (processed by `ExpressionConverter`) resolves to an empty string at runtime, causing the gRPC subscriber to fail silently and miss all staking reward-related topic messages while the monitor reports healthy status?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/subscribe/SubscribeProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: validate()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the `@Max(1024)` constraint on `clients` to set exactly 1024 clients, each subscribing to a different non-staking REST endpoint, exhausting the connection pool and preventing any staking reward monitoring subscriber from obtaining a client, enabling theft of unpaid rewards during the resource exhaustion?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/subscribe/SubscribeProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: validate()] Can an unprivileged external user configure a `RestSubscriberProperties` with `subscribers=1024` (the `@Max(1024)` limit) to create 1024 concurrent REST subscriber threads all querying the mirror node, causing a denial-of-service on the mirror node's staking reward endpoints and preventing legitimate reward monitoring?
